title: Epigram Printed In The Northampton Mercury.
content:
To purify their wine, some people bleed
    A lamb into the barrel, and succeed;
    No nostrum, planters say, is half so good
    To make fine sugar as a negro�s blood.
    Now lambs and negroes both are harmless things,
    And thence perhaps this wondrous virtue springs,
    �Tis in the blood of innocence alone�
    Good cause why planters never try their own.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "Epigram Printed In The Northampton Mercury."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
